channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: An attack targeting a Turkish defense company leaves 4 dead and 14 wounded
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Assailants set off explosives and opened fire in an attack Wednesday on the premises of the Turkish state-run aerospace and defense company TUSAS, killing four people and wounding several, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes pound Lebanese coastal city after residents evacuate
item.description: TYRE, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli jets struck multiple buildings in Lebanon's southern coastal city of Tyre on Wednesday, sending up large clouds of black smoke, while Hezbollah confirmed that a top official widely expected to be the militant group's next leader had been killed in an Israeli strike.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMED ZAATARI and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary's Orbán claims the EU seeks to topple his government as his hostility toward it grows
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán claimed in a speech on Wednesday that the European Union seeks to topple his government and install a puppet regime in the Central European country, an escalation of open hostility toward the bloc by the member considered to have the war
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Charles gets a warm welcome on his first visit as king to Samoa. But uneasy questions await
item.description: SIUMU, Samoa (AP) — In the dark, dozens of people lined a narrow road near the Samoan fishing village of Siumu, gathering near a sign bearing a portrait of King Charles III and waiting excitedly for a glimpse of the real monarch.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and AYAKA McGILL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Group of metal detectorists uncover hoard of coins in England
item.description: A group of metal detectorists discovered a hoard of 21 coins near Okehampton Castle nearly a year ago, but the news of their finding was just recently unveiled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus sets a Jan. 26 election that's almost certain to extend its authoritarian leader's rule
item.description: Lawmakers in Belarus set the next presidential election for Jan. 26, a vote almost certain to extend the three-decade rule of authoritarian leader Alexander Lukashenko.
item.dc:creator: Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Britain and Germany sign defense pact to counter Russia's growing threat
item.description: LONDON (AP) — German submarine-hunting planes will patrol the North Atlantic from a base in Scotland under a new Britain-Germany defense pact sealed in response to the growing threat from Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: US scrambles after Israel war plan leak: What to know
item.description: An apparent U.S. intelligence breach related to Israel's plans to strike Iran has unsettled the intelligence community and sent the Biden administration scrambling to determine if it came from a hack or an internal leak.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: US defense chief says there’s evidence North Korea has sent troops to Russia
item.description: The U.S. defense secretary says there's evidence that North Korea has sent troops to Russia and calls it a “very, very serious issue” if they join the war in Ukraine on Moscow’s side.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Desperation amid search for survivors of an airstrike on a crowded area near major Beirut hospital
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Nearly 16 hours after an Israeli airstrike hit across the street from Beirut’s main public hospital, rescuers were still removing debris Tuesday from the overcrowded slum area.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Floods in Philippines kill at least 9 and trap others on roofs as storm approaches
item.description: Torrential rain set off by an approaching storm has swamped eastern Philippine cities and towns in widespread floods that have killed at least nine people, trapped others on their roofs and sparked frantic appeals for help.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Police bust restaurant serving ‘best-selling’ pizza with side of cocaine
item.description: German police have raided a Düsseldorf pizzeria for allegedly serving pies with a side of cocaine. The restaurant's 36-year-old manager was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walmart employee found dead inside walk-in oven at Canada store: police
item.description: Authorities in Canada are continuing an investigation into the death of a Walmart employee who was found inside a walk-in oven at the store.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard officer charged in plot to kill American author in US
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment against four Iranian officials Tuesday for their alleged involvement in a murder-for-hire targeting Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Cuba fights to come back from a nationwide blackout, then a storm that killed six
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A small town in far eastern Cuba was recovering Tuesday from flooding that killed at least six people after Oscar crossed the island’s eastern coast as a tropical storm with winds and heavy rain.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ and MILEXSY DURAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France defends restrictions on Israeli firms supplying Middle East wars and says it's not a boycott
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France's government on Tuesday defended its decision to bar Israeli companies supplying the wars in the Middle East from exhibiting at an upcoming trade fair outside Paris. Organizers of the Nov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine blasts UN chief over potential meeting with Putin at BRICS, says stayed away from Kyiv's peace summit
item.description: Ukraine blasted U.N. Secretary-General Guterres for allegedly agreeing to meet with Russian President Putin during the BRICS summit as leaders from the West's top adversaries like China and Iran convene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 transgender women stabbed to death at home in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two men armed with daggers killed two transgender women at their home in conservative northwest Pakistan overnight before fleeing the scene, police said, a sign of increasing violence against trans people in the country.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says only a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway a reluctant Germany
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his so-called “victory plan” after the U.S. election, but concedes that its key demand — an invitation to join NATO — is not welcome by some major Western partners, in particular Germany.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Binational delegation heads to Mexico City to talk sewage, border waits, stalled port of entry
item.description: The delegation is led by the San Diego Regional Chamber of Commerce, which says their mission is to "champion U.S.-Mexico relations and advance issues critical to the success of the binational business community as the newly sworn-in Mexico Administration begins its session.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken heads to Middle East in wake of Sinwar's killing
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is going to the Middle East in the wake of the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: South Korea demands withdrawal of North Korean troops allegedly helping Russia fight Ukraine
item.description: South Korea issued a demand to Russia to condemn the cooperation between Russia and North Korea, calling for the withdrawal of North Korean troops from Russia's war with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar dumps heavy rain on Cuba as it heads toward the Bahamas
item.description: Its maximum sustained winds blew at 40 mph on Monday as Oscar moved west-northwest at 3 mph. The storm was located about 15 miles (25 kilometers) west-northwest of Guantánamo, Cuba, the U.S. National Hurricane Center in Miami said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American reportedly kidnapped in Philippine coastal town; police investigating
item.description: Police in the Philippines are searching for American national Elliot Onil Eastman, from Vermont, after receiving reports gunmen had kidnapped him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paul Whelan describes 'torture' of Russian prison
item.description: Former Marine Paul Whelan described the “torture” he endured while being held captive in a Russian prison for years. “The Russian government wanted to put pressure on the United States by treating me badly. Sleep deprivation is considered torture,” Whelan said Sunday on CBS’s “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Cuba hit by second blackout after grid collapse
item.description: Cubans experienced their second black out wave Saturday after government officials said they were working to restore electrical service to hospitals and crucial service centers after a grid failure on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Japan's governing party headquarters is attacked with firebombs and suspect is arrested
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s governing party in Tokyo on Saturday, then crashed his car into the fencing of the prime minister's residence, police said. There were no reports of injuries.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Strike on Netanyahu home 'a signal' Iran proxies not incapacitated, expert says
item.description: A recent drone strike targeting the home of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending "a signal" that Iran-backed militant groups are not "incapacitated," despite losing senior leadership in recent weeks, according to a Middle East expert. Daniel Levy, the president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: US long-range B-2 stealth bombers target underground bunkers of Yemen's Houthi rebels
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. military unleashed B-2 stealth bombers to target underground bunkers used by Yemen’s Houthi rebels early Thursday, a major escalation in the American response to the rebels' attacks on Mideast shipping lanes that appeared to be a warning to Iran as well.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 27 in Lebanon, including in a town with a dark history of civilian deaths
item.description: QANA, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes pounded areas across Lebanon, killing at least 27 people over the past 24 hours, officials said Wednesday, including more than a dozen in a southern town where Israeli bombardments in previous conflicts are seared into local memory.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD ZAATARI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation
